macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Major Indices Plunge to Multi-Month Lows Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Rising Oil Prices
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 1.7% to 6,368.85, Dow Jones dropped 1.7% to 45,166.64, and Nasdaq Composite lost 2.2% to 20,948.36, all hitting multi-month lows due to escalating Iran tensions and oil-driven inflation risks. VIX surged above 30, signaling heightened volatility.

items.title: Tech and Financial Stocks Lead Market Declines; Nvidia Down 2.2%, Amazon Drops 4%
items.summary: Megacap tech stocks like Nvidia (-2.2%) and Amazon (-4.0%) faced pressure, while Carnival cut its annual profit outlook (-4.3%) and Goldman Sachs slipped 2.4%, dragging financials lower amid broader equity weakness.

items.title: Trump Suggests US Seizure of Iran's Oil Exports as Markets Enter Correction Territory
items.summary: President Trump stated preference to 'take the oil' from Iran, potentially targeting Kharg Island, amid ongoing war; Nasdaq and Dow fell into correction territory, with S&P 500 also losing ground and Russell 2000 deepening its correction.

items.title: Asian Markets Mixed Ahead of Sharp Monday Weakness on Geopolitical Escalation
items.summary: Japan’s Nikkei 225 fell 0.4% to 53,373.07, South Korea’s Kospi slipped 0.4% to 5,300.61, while Hong Kong’s Hang Seng rose 0.4% to 24,951.88 and China’s CSI 300 gained 0.6% to 4,502.57 before broader declines.

items.title: Weekly Losses: S&P 500 Down Over 2%, Nasdaq Over 3% as Volatility Persists
items.summary: Major US indices recorded weekly declines with S&P 500 down over 2% to 6,368, Dow about 1% to 45,166, and Nasdaq over 3%, driven primarily by geopolitics and energy prices rather than company news.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Ethereum Price Surges to $2,070.90 with $70 Daily Gain
items.summary: Ethereum (ETH) reached $2,070.90 at 8:45 a.m. ET on March 30, 2026, marking a $70.70 increase from yesterday and over 80% gains earlier in the year despite volatility.[1]

items.title: Bitcoin Climbs to $67,822.72, Up $1,026 from Yesterday
items.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) hit $67,822.72 at 8:45 a.m. ET on March 30, 2026, reflecting a $1,026.71 daily rise, though down from last year's peak amid ongoing market swings.[2]

items.title: Cryptocurrency Funds Pro-Russia Drone Purchases on Blockchains
items.summary: Pro-Russia groups raised millions in crypto across blockchains to buy UAVs and parts; blockchain analysis reveals sanctioned suppliers like KB Vostok and state-adjacent networks.[3]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: U.S. Intelligence Elevates Quantum Computing to Same Threat Level as AI
items.summary: The 2026 Annual Threat Assessment treats quantum computing as a major national security challenge on par with AI, highlighting both as defining technological threats to U.S. security.

items.title: FPT Wins AI & Machine Learning Innovation Award for iSuite Platform
items.summary: FPT Software received the AI & Machine Learning Innovation Award at the 2026 InsurInnovator Connect Asia Awards for iSuite, an AI-native digital insurance platform that automates claims, boosts straight-through processing to over 70%, and enhances fraud detection.

items.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Claims AGI Has Been Achieved Amid Ongoing Debate
items.summary: Nvidia's Jensen Huang stated on a podcast that artificial general intelligence (AGI) has already been achieved, sparking debate; Google DeepMind's new cognitive taxonomy paper reveals current AI models like GPT-5 score only 57% on AGI benchmarks, with a $200,000 contest launched for better evaluations.

items.title: AI Adoption Gap Widens Between U.S. and Europe, Boosting U.S. Productivity
items.summary: 43% of U.S. workers use AI in 2026 compared to 32% in Europe, contributing to 3.2 percentage points of additional U.S. productivity growth since 2022 and exacerbating the transatlantic productivity divide.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Indian Air Force Launches Project Vayu Baan, India's First Helicopter-Launched Drone System
items.summary: The Indian Air Force initiated Project Vayu Baan, India's inaugural helicopter-launched Unmanned Aerial System (UAS) for surveillance and precision strikes, led by the IAF Directorate of Aerospace Design with an RFP issued in March 2026. The system features EO/IR sensors, real-time video transmission, and resilience against GPS jamming, positioning India alongside the US and China in air-launched drone technology.[2]

items.title: SK Hynix Plans US Stock Listing Amid Semiconductor Pressures
items.summary: South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix is set to pursue a US listing, as highlighted in emerging markets updates, while facing short-term challenges from Google's TurboQuant technology impacting Asian semiconductor firms like Samsung and SK Hynix.[3]

items.title: Indian Railways Launches SHINE App for Workplace Safety
items.summary: Indian Railways introduced the SHINE app on March 8, 2026, to enhance workplace safety and empower women employees, marking a key initiative for a secure work environment.[2]

items.title: AWS Glitch Disrupts Customer Experience Across the Internet
items.summary: An AWS outage caused widespread disruptions to customer experiences online, underscoring vulnerabilities in cloud infrastructure as enterprises rethink service management strategies for 2026.[5]

items.title: Oracle’s TikTok Outages Highlight CX Platform Risks
items.summary: Outages at Oracle's TikTok services exposed hidden risks in customer experience platforms, prompting enterprises to prioritize CX infrastructure monitoring for owned and unowned networks.[5]
